rdfs:label: PhysWorldLM Radar Relationship Ontology (PWMO-REL)
rdfs:comment: Canonical relationship graph defining every semantic, structural, functional, physical, operational, C2, electromagnetic, EW, maintenance, logistics, AI, and temporal relationship between all radar entities in PhysWorldLM.

1_Structural.rdfs:label: Structural Relationships
1_Structural.skos:definition: Relationships describing physical composition, containment, mounting, and assembly of radar entities.

hasComponent.rdfs:label: hasComponent
hasComponent.skos:definition: Asserts that the subject entity physically or logically contains the object entity as a constituent component.
constraints: Object must be typed as pwmo:RadarComponent or subclass
validationRules: No radar may assert hasComponent of itself (anti-reflexive)

componentOf.rdfs:label: componentOf
componentOf.skos:definition: Inverse of hasComponent. The subject is a constituent part of the object system.

partOf.rdfs:label: partOf
partOf.skos:definition: Weaker mereological relationship; the subject is a physical part of the object but may not be a replaceable component (e.g., a waveguide slot is part of a slotted array).

contains.rdfs:label: contains
contains.skos:definition: Spatial containment; the subject physically encloses the object.

assembledFrom.rdfs:label: assembledFrom
assembledFrom.skos:definition: Manufacturing assembly relationship; the subject system is assembled from the enumerated object parts.

mountedOn.rdfs:label: mountedOn
mountedOn.skos:definition: Physical mounting relationship; the subject is mechanically mounted on the object structure.

integratedInto.rdfs:label: integratedInto
integratedInto.skos:definition: Denotes deep functional and physical integration into the host structure, beyond simple mounting — the entity and host share structural members, cooling, or power planes.

housedIn.rdfs:label: housedIn
housedIn.skos:definition: The subject component is physically located inside the object shelter, radome, or chassis.

connectedTo.rdfs:label: connectedTo
connectedTo.skos:definition: General bidirectional physical or electrical connection. Subject and object are linked via cable, waveguide, fiber, or bus.

poweredBy.rdfs:label: poweredBy
poweredBy.skos:definition: The subject receives electrical power from the object power supply or generator.

cooledBy.rdfs:label: cooledBy
cooledBy.skos:definition: The subject component dissipates waste heat through the object cooling system.

protectedBy.rdfs:label: protectedBy
protectedBy.skos:definition: The subject entity is physically or electronically protected by the object (radome, limiter, armor, ECCM module).

stabilizedBy.rdfs:label: stabilizedBy
stabilizedBy.skos:definition: The subject antenna or sensor is attitude-stabilized by the object stabilization platform.

replacedBy.rdfs:label: replacedBy
replacedBy.skos:definition: Maintenance relationship; the subject LRU is replaced by the object spare unit during field maintenance.

interfacesVia.rdfs:label: interfacesVia
interfacesVia.skos:definition: The subject communicates with the object through the specified interface standard.

2_RF_Electromagnetic.rdfs:label: RF and Electromagnetic Relationships
2_RF_Electromagnetic.skos:definition: Relationships describing RF signal flow, electromagnetic propagation, and wave interactions.

generatesSignal.rdfs:label: generatesSignal
generatesSignal.skos:definition: The subject device generates the RF or IF signal consumed by the object device.

transmitsTo.rdfs:label: transmitsTo
transmitsTo.skos:definition: The subject transmitter or antenna emits RF energy toward the object target or region.

receives.rdfs:label: receives
receives.skos:definition: The subject antenna or receiver captures RF energy from the object source or reflected target.

amplifies.rdfs:label: amplifies
amplifies.skos:definition: The subject amplifier increases the power of the signal from the object.

filters.rdfs:label: filters
filters.skos:definition: The subject filter passes or rejects frequency components from the signal produced by the object.

mixes.rdfs:label: mixes
mixes.skos:definition: The subject mixer multiplies the RF signal with a local oscillator signal to produce an IF signal.

digitizes.rdfs:label: digitizes
digitizes.skos:definition: The ADC converts the analog signal from the object into digital samples.

modulates.rdfs:label: modulates
modulates.skos:definition: The waveform generator impresses modulation onto the carrier signal (LFM, FMCW, BPSK, OFDM, etc.).

demodulates.rdfs:label: demodulates
demodulates.skos:definition: The receiver or digital down-converter extracts the baseband information from the modulated signal.

propagatesThrough.rdfs:label: propagatesThrough
propagatesThrough.skos:definition: The RF signal travels through the specified propagation medium.

attenuatedBy.rdfs:label: attenuatedBy
attenuatedBy.skos:definition: The RF signal loses power when passing through or reflecting from the specified medium or material.

reflectedBy.rdfs:label: reflectedBy
reflectedBy.skos:definition: The incident RF signal is reflected by the target surface back toward the radar.

scatteredBy.rdfs:label: scatteredBy
scatteredBy.skos:definition: The RF signal is scattered diffusely by the object (volume clutter, rain, chaff cloud, rough surface).

absorbedBy.rdfs:label: absorbedBy
absorbedBy.skos:definition: RF energy is absorbed by the object (RAM coating, absorbing material, ionosphere).

generatesBeam.rdfs:label: generatesBeam
generatesBeam.skos:definition: The antenna forms and directs a focused electromagnetic beam with specified parameters.

steeresBeamTo.rdfs:label: steersBeamTo
steeresBeamTo.skos:definition: The beamforming network electronically steers the radiation pattern to the specified direction.

illuminates.rdfs:label: illuminates
illuminates.skos:definition: The fire control radar continuously illuminates the target with RF energy to support semi-active missile homing.

isolatesRFPath.rdfs:label: isolatesRFPath
isolatesRFPath.skos:definition: The subject device (circulator, duplexer, T/R switch) provides RF isolation between TX and RX paths.

3_SignalProcessing.rdfs:label: Signal Processing Relationships
3_SignalProcessing.skos:definition: Relationships describing the signal processing chain from raw ADC data to extracted target reports.

processesSignal.rdfs:label: processesSignal
processesSignal.skos:definition: The subject processing block operates on the digital signal received from the object.

performsPulseCompression.rdfs:label: performsPulseCompression
performsPulseCompression.skos:definition: The matched filter or correlation block compresses the received wideband waveform to achieve range resolution.

performsFFT.rdfs:label: performsFFT
performsFFT.skos:definition: The signal processor applies a Fast Fourier Transform to the pulse-compressed data to extract Doppler frequency bins.

performsCFAR.rdfs:label: performsCFAR
performsCFAR.skos:definition: The CFAR detector adaptively sets detection threshold relative to estimated local noise/clutter level.

performsMTI.rdfs:label: performsMTI
performsMTI.skos:definition: The MTI filter cancels stationary clutter returns by applying delay-line cancellation, suppressing zero-Doppler returns.

performsMTD.rdfs:label: performsMTD
performsMTD.skos:definition: The MTD processor uses a bank of Doppler filters (FFT-based) to separate moving targets from clutter across all velocity bins simultaneously.

performsSTAP.rdfs:label: performsSTAP
performsSTAP.skos:definition: Space-Time Adaptive Processing jointly processes multi-channel (spatial) and multi-pulse (temporal) data to suppress airborne clutter and detect slow ground movers.
performsSTAP.computationalComplexity: O(K^3) per range cell, K = space-time degrees of freedom

performsBeamforming.rdfs:label: performsBeamforming
performsBeamforming.skos:definition: The beamforming unit applies complex weights to antenna element signals to form directional beams.

performsSARImaging.rdfs:label: performsSARImaging
performsSARImaging.skos:definition: The SAR processor integrates coherent returns across the synthetic aperture to form a 2D image.

performsMotionCompensation.rdfs:label: performsMotionCompensation
performsMotionCompensation.skos:definition: The motion compensation module corrects SAR phase history for deviations from ideal straight-line flight using INS/GPS data.

estimatesRange.rdfs:label: estimatesRange
estimatesRange.skos:definition: The radar processor measures range to the target using two-way propagation time-of-flight.

estimatesVelocity.rdfs:label: estimatesVelocity
estimatesVelocity.skos:definition: The Doppler processor extracts radial velocity of target from measured Doppler frequency shift.

estimatesAngle.rdfs:label: estimatesAngle
estimatesAngle.skos:definition: The monopulse processor measures target angular position (azimuth and elevation) from sum and difference beam ratios.

estimatesRCS.rdfs:label: estimatesRCS
estimatesRCS.skos:definition: The radar processor estimates target radar cross section from calibrated received power, range, and system parameters.

performsDespeckle.rdfs:label: performsDespeckle
performsDespeckle.skos:definition: The despeckling module suppresses multiplicative speckle noise in SAR imagery using filtering, statistical, or deep learning approaches.
performsDespeckle.note: Directly relevant to PhysWorldLM SAR-DDPM / StructDiff USF framework

fusesData.rdfs:label: fusesData
fusesData.skos:definition: The fusion processor combines track data from the subject and additional sensors to produce a higher-confidence composite track.

4_Detection_Tracking.rdfs:label: Detection and Tracking Relationships
4_Detection_Tracking.skos:definition: Relationships describing the radar's detection, tracking, identification, and classification of targets.

detects.rdfs:label: detects
detects.skos:definition: The radar system produces a detection report for the specified target type, exceeding detection threshold at a given probability.

tracks.rdfs:label: tracks
tracks.skos:definition: The radar initiates and maintains a track file on the target, providing continuous state estimates (position, velocity, acceleration).

initiatesTrack.rdfs:label: initiatesTrack
initiatesTrack.skos:definition: The radar creates a new track file upon confirmed detection of a new target.

losesTrack.rdfs:label: losesTrack
losesTrack.skos:definition: The radar drops the track file due to insufficient detections, jamming, or target maneuver exceeding gate.

reacquiresTarget.rdfs:label: reacquiresTarget
reacquiresTarget.skos:definition: The radar re-establishes a track on a previously lost target.

identifies.rdfs:label: identifies
identifies.skos:definition: The radar and IFF system determine the identity (friend/neutral/foe) of the tracked target.

classifies.rdfs:label: classifies
classifies.skos:definition: The radar signal processor classifies the target into a type category using wideband signature, micro-Doppler, or ML-based NCTR.

prioritizesThreat.rdfs:label: prioritizesThreat
prioritizesThreat.skos:definition: The mission computer assigns a threat priority rank to each tracked target based on lethality, engagement timeline, and RoE.

cues.rdfs:label: cues
cues.skos:definition: The subject radar provides initial spatial cue (bearing, range, altitude) to the object sensor or weapon system to accelerate its search.

handoffTarget.rdfs:label: handoffTarget
handoffTarget.skos:definition: The subject radar transfers track custody to the object radar or weapon system for precision engagement.
handoffTarget.handoffData: complete track state vector + covariance matrix + classification + IFF

designates.rdfs:label: designates
designates.skos:definition: The radar laser or RF designates a specific target for precision munition terminal guidance.

surveils.rdfs:label: surveils
surveils.skos:definition: The radar provides persistent surveillance coverage of an airspace volume or ground area.

5_CommandControl.rdfs:label: Command and Control Relationships
5_CommandControl.skos:definition: Relationships describing C2 authority, data flows, and tasking between radar systems and command networks.

controlledBy.rdfs:label: controlledBy
controlledBy.skos:definition: The subject radar is operated and tasked by the object operator, C2 system, or command authority.

reportsTo.rdfs:label: reportsTo
reportsTo.skos:definition: The radar sends processed tracks and reports up the C2 hierarchy to the object command node.

coordinatesWith.rdfs:label: coordinatesWith
coordinatesWith.skos:definition: Peer-level coordination between radar systems for deconfliction, hand-off, or cooperative search.

sendsTrackDataTo.rdfs:label: sendsTrackDataTo
sendsTrackDataTo.skos:definition: The radar transmits formatted track data (position, velocity, classification, IFF) to the object system.

receivesCommandFrom.rdfs:label: receivesCommandFrom
receivesCommandFrom.skos:definition: The radar receives mode change, tasking, or targeting commands from the object C2 node.

exchangesTracksWith.rdfs:label: exchangesTracksWith
exchangesTracksWith.skos:definition: Bidirectional track sharing between radar nodes for netted air picture compilation.

networkedWith.rdfs:label: networkedWith
networkedWith.skos:definition: The radar is part of a wider sensor network enabling distributed surveillance and netted fire control.

assignedTo.rdfs:label: assignedTo
assignedTo.skos:definition: The radar resource is assigned to support the specified mission, unit, or weapon system.

synchronizedWith.rdfs:label: synchronizedWith
synchronizedWith.skos:definition: Timing or frequency synchronization between radar nodes for coherent operation (netted radar, bistatic radar).

6_WeaponEngagement.rdfs:label: Weapon and Engagement Relationships
6_WeaponEngagement.skos:definition: Relationships between radar systems and weapon systems across the engagement sequence.

supportsFireControl.rdfs:label: supportsFireControl
supportsFireControl.skos:definition: The radar provides precision targeting data sufficient for weapon engagement.

guidesMissile.rdfs:label: guidesMissile
guidesMissile.skos:definition: The radar provides mid-course or terminal guidance commands or illumination to the object missile.

supportsCIWS.rdfs:label: supportsCIWS
supportsCIWS.skos:definition: The tracking radar provides real-time fire control for Close-In Weapon System engagement against terminal threats.

engagesTarget.rdfs:label: engagesTarget
engagesTarget.skos:definition: The weapon system, cued and guided by the radar, launches weapon against the target.

providesTargetIllumination.rdfs:label: providesTargetIllumination
providesTargetIllumination.skos:definition: The continuous wave illuminator transmits RF energy at the target to support seeker homing of SARH missiles.

assignsWeapon.rdfs:label: assignsWeapon
assignsWeapon.skos:definition: The radar-cued C2 system assigns a specific weapon or interceptor to engage the tracked threat.

evaluatesKillProbability.rdfs:label: evaluatesKillProbability
evaluatesKillProbability.skos:definition: The fire control system computes Pk (probability of kill) for a proposed weapon-target pair based on radar-derived track quality.

7_ElectronicWarfare.rdfs:label: Electronic Warfare Relationships
7_ElectronicWarfare.skos:definition: Relationships describing offensive and defensive EW interactions between radars and jamming systems.

jammedBy.rdfs:label: jammedBy
jammedBy.skos:definition: The subject radar's reception is degraded by the object jamming system (noise, deceptive, or repeater jamming).

jams.rdfs:label: jams
jams.skos:definition: The subject EW system actively transmits to deny, degrade, or deceive the object radar.

homeOnJam.rdfs:label: homeOnJam
homeOnJam.skos:definition: The anti-radiation missile or HOJ mode allows the radar or missile to use the enemy jamming signal as a targeting beacon.

detectsJamming.rdfs:label: detectsJamming
detectsJamming.skos:definition: The radar or its ECCM subsystem detects the presence of jamming on the radar frequency.

performsFrequencyHopping.rdfs:label: performsFrequencyHopping
performsFrequencyHopping.skos:definition: The radar changes operating frequency pseudo-randomly pulse-to-pulse to defeat narrowband jamming and reduce LPI.

performsAdaptiveNulling.rdfs:label: performsAdaptiveNulling
performsAdaptiveNulling.skos:definition: The AESA or DPCA beamformer steers a null in the antenna pattern toward the jamming source to suppress its effect.

performsLPI.rdfs:label: performsLPI
performsLPI.skos:definition: The radar employs Low Probability of Intercept techniques to reduce probability of detection by ESM systems.

spoofedBy.rdfs:label: spoofedBy
spoofedBy.skos:definition: The radar is deceived by a false target injected by the object DRFM or deception jammer.

protectedByECCM.rdfs:label: protectedByECCM
protectedByECCM.skos:definition: The radar is hardened against EW attack through the object ECCM measure set.

threatenedByARM.rdfs:label: threatenedByARM
threatenedByARM.skos:definition: The emitting radar is potentially targeted by the object Anti-Radiation Missile homing on its emissions.

performsElectronicAttack.rdfs:label: performsElectronicAttack
performsElectronicAttack.skos:definition: A dual-role AESA radar uses its transmit aperture to actively jam, deceive, or disrupt enemy electronics.
performsElectronicAttack.capability: AN/APG-77, AN/APG-81 EA modes; EPAWSS integration

8_Platform.rdfs:label: Platform Relationships
8_Platform.skos:definition: Relationships describing radar installation and integration with carrier platforms.

installedOn.rdfs:label: installedOn
installedOn.skos:definition: The radar system is installed on and carried by the specified platform.

deployedFrom.rdfs:label: deployedFrom
deployedFrom.skos:definition: The radar is carried to operational site by the object transport and deployed there.

supportsPlatformNavigation.rdfs:label: supportsPlatformNavigation
supportsPlatformNavigation.skos:definition: The radar provides terrain-following, terrain-avoidance, or navigation cues to the host platform.

sharesPlatformPower.rdfs:label: sharesPlatformPower
sharesPlatformPower.skos:definition: The radar competes with other platform systems for electrical power from the shared generator or APU.

sharesPlatformCooling.rdfs:label: sharesPlatformCooling
sharesPlatformCooling.skos:definition: The radar uses the host platform's liquid cooling loop or ECS for heat rejection.

9_Environment.rdfs:label: Environmental Relationships
9_Environment.skos:definition: Relationships between radar systems and atmospheric, terrain, and clutter environments.

affectedByRain.rdfs:label: affectedByRain
affectedByRain.skos:definition: Rainfall causes both signal attenuation and volume clutter that degrades radar performance.

affectedBySeaClutter.rdfs:label: affectedBySeaClutter
affectedBySeaClutter.skos:definition: Sea surface backscatter competes with low-flying target returns in naval radars.

affectedByGroundClutter.rdfs:label: affectedByGroundClutter
affectedByGroundClutter.skos:definition: Surface backscatter from land masks low-altitude targets and requires MTI/STAP to suppress.

affectedByAtmosphericDucting.rdfs:label: affectedByAtmosphericDucting
affectedByAtmosphericDucting.skos:definition: Anomalous propagation (ducting) can extend radar range beyond line-of-sight or create blind zones.

affectedByMultipath.rdfs:label: affectedByMultipath
affectedByMultipath.skos:definition: Multipath propagation over sea or flat terrain causes constructive/destructive interference, creating lobing in elevation coverage.

affectedByIonosphere.rdfs:label: affectedByIonosphere
affectedByIonosphere.skos:definition: Ionospheric layers refract, absorb, and scatter HF and UHF signals, enabling OTH radar but causing errors in BMD radars.

compensatesForClutter.rdfs:label: compensatesForClutter
compensatesForClutter.skos:definition: The radar signal processor actively compensates for or suppresses the effect of the specified clutter type.

10_Maintenance_Logistics.rdfs:label: Maintenance and Logistics Relationships
10_Maintenance_Logistics.skos:definition: Relationships describing maintenance, supply chain, and support activities for radar systems.

maintainedBy.rdfs:label: maintainedBy
maintainedBy.skos:definition: The radar system is maintained by the specified maintenance unit or technician echelon.

calibratedBy.rdfs:label: calibratedBy
calibratedBy.skos:definition: The radar or component is periodically calibrated by the object test equipment or reference standard.

diagnosedBy.rdfs:label: diagnosedBy
diagnosedBy.skos:definition: The BIT system or test equipment diagnoses fault condition in the subject radar LRU.

upgradedBy.rdfs:label: upgradedBy
upgradedBy.skos:definition: The radar capability is enhanced through installation of the object upgrade kit (hardware or software).

suppliedBy.rdfs:label: suppliedBy
suppliedBy.skos:definition: Spare parts, consumables, or support equipment are supplied to the radar unit by the object logistics organization.

testedBy.rdfs:label: testedBy
testedBy.skos:definition: The subject radar or component undergoes functional test by the object test system.

storedAt.rdfs:label: storedAt
storedAt.skos:definition: The radar system or spare part is stored at the specified location when not in operational service.

hasSuccessorSystem.rdfs:label: hasSuccessorSystem
hasSuccessorSystem.skos:definition: The subject radar system is replaced by or succeeded by the object radar in the operational inventory.

11_Navigation_Timing.rdfs:label: Navigation and Timing Relationships
11_Navigation_Timing.skos:definition: Relationships between radar systems and navigation, positioning, and timing infrastructure.

referencedByGPS.rdfs:label: referencedByGPS
referencedByGPS.skos:definition: The radar uses GPS timing signal for coherent pulse-to-pulse phase reference and geo-location.

compensatedByINS.rdfs:label: compensatedByINS
compensatedByINS.skos:definition: The radar uses INS platform motion data for motion compensation in SAR focusing and antenna beam stabilization.

georeferencesWith.rdfs:label: georeferencesWith
georeferencesWith.skos:definition: The radar output is geo-referenced to absolute Earth coordinates using the specified navigation system.

12_Communication_DataLink.rdfs:label: Communication and Data Link Relationships
12_Communication_DataLink.skos:definition: Relationships between radar systems and communication networks, data links, and interoperability standards.

communicatesVia.rdfs:label: communicatesVia
communicatesVia.skos:definition: The radar exchanges data with external systems through the specified communication interface.

MADL.note: F-35 inter-flight LPI link; 1 Gbps encrypted

ASTERIX.note: Eurocontrol ATM data format for civil/military radar

MIL-STD-1553B.note: Avionics bus; radar to aircraft systems

publishesTracksTo.rdfs:label: publishesTracksTo
publishesTracksTo.skos:definition: The radar publishes track messages to the object network node or publish-subscribe middleware.

subscribesToTrackData.rdfs:label: subscribesToTrackData
subscribesToTrackData.skos:definition: The system consumes track data published by the specified radar source node.

interoperableWith.rdfs:label: interoperableWith
interoperableWith.skos:definition: The radar system is interoperable with the specified allied or coalition system through common standards.

13_AI_Cognitive.rdfs:label: AI and Cognitive Radar Relationships
13_AI_Cognitive.skos:definition: Relationships describing AI-driven control, optimization, and autonomous decision-making in radar systems.

classifiedByAI.rdfs:label: classifiedByAI
classifiedByAI.skos:definition: An AI model classifies the target type or track from radar signature data.

optimizedByAI.rdfs:label: optimizedByAI
optimizedByAI.skos:definition: An AI optimization module dynamically adjusts radar parameters for optimal performance.

scheduledByAI.rdfs:label: scheduledByAI
scheduledByAI.skos:definition: An AI beam and resource scheduler manages radar dwell times, beam directions, and waveform selection.

adaptedByCognitiveLoop.rdfs:label: adaptedByCognitiveLoop
adaptedByCognitiveLoop.skos:definition: The radar waveform and processing are adapted in a closed perception-action loop based on environmental observations.

anomalyDetectedByAI.rdfs:label: anomalyDetectedByAI
anomalyDetectedByAI.skos:definition: An AI module detects anomalous radar behaviour indicating hardware fault, jamming, spoofing, or cyber intrusion.

fusedByAI.rdfs:label: fusedByAI
fusedByAI.skos:definition: AI-based multi-source data fusion combines radar, EO/IR, ESM, and AIS data to produce composite track.

despeckledByDiffusionModel.rdfs:label: despeckledByDiffusionModel
despeckledByDiffusionModel.skos:definition: The SAR image is denoised/despeckled by a score-based diffusion model (DDPM/DDIM) conditioned on structural features.
despeckledByDiffusionModel.note: Direct link to PhysWorldLM StructDiff / USF SAR despeckling framework

14_Temporal.rdfs:label: Temporal Relationships
14_Temporal.skos:definition: Relationships describing temporal ordering, dependencies, and state transitions in radar operation.

precedes.rdfs:label: precedes
precedes.skos:definition: The subject event or process temporally precedes the object event.

follows.rdfs:label: follows
follows.skos:definition: The subject event temporally follows the object event.

concurrentWith.rdfs:label: concurrentWith
concurrentWith.skos:definition: The subject and object processes execute simultaneously or within the same radar pulse interval.
examples: AESA multi-beam simultaneous search and track beams
Simultaneous TX waveform generation and prior-pulse RX processing

triggeredBy.rdfs:label: triggeredBy
triggeredBy.skos:definition: The subject mode or process is triggered by the occurrence of the object event or condition.

dependsOnCompletion.rdfs:label: dependsOnCompletion
dependsOnCompletion.skos:definition: The subject process cannot begin until the object process has completed successfully.

hasOperationalState.rdfs:label: hasOperationalState
hasOperationalState.skos:definition: The radar currently occupies the specified operational state.

transitionsTo.rdfs:label: transitionsTo
transitionsTo.skos:definition: The radar transitions from its current state to the target state upon the specified trigger.

15_Inheritance_Taxonomy.rdfs:label: Inheritance and Taxonomy Relationships
15_Inheritance_Taxonomy.skos:definition: Relationships describing ontological class hierarchy and specialization.

inheritsFrom.rdfs:label: inheritsFrom
inheritsFrom.skos:definition: The subject class inherits all properties and relationships of the object parent class (OWL rdfs:subClassOf).

specializes.rdfs:label: specializes
specializes.skos:definition: The subject is a more specific subtype of the object class with additional constraints or capabilities.

instanceOf.rdfs:label: instanceOf
instanceOf.skos:definition: The subject individual is a concrete instance of the object class (OWL rdf:type).

RelationshipRegistry.rdfs:label: Complete Relationship ID Registry
RelationshipRegistry.skos:definition: Flat index of all relationship IDs for rapid lookup, graph database import, and validation.

RepresentativeRelationshipTriples.rdfs:label: Representative Relationship Triple Examples
RepresentativeRelationshipTriples.skos:definition: Canonical example triples demonstrating correct use of the relationship ontology across all categories.

rules.description: Every relationship triple must have a subject and object typed in upper_taxonomy.json, component_ontology.json, or rf_signal_processing.json
No structural containment cycle: X hasComponent Y hasComponent ... hasComponent X is forbidden
installedOn subject must be a Radar or RadarSubsystem; object must be a Platform
guidesMissile is only valid if the subject is a FireControlRadar or IlluminatorRadar
performsSTAP requires subject to be a STAPProcessor or have STAPProcessor as a component
despeckledByDiffusionModel subject must be a DiffusionModel; object must be a SAR_Image or SpeckleNoise type
Temporal precedes/follows must not form cycles (irreflexive, asymmetric)
All relationship IDs must resolve in the RelationshipRegistry
communicatesVia interface values must match a declared interface type in DataLink component ontology
